commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Human follow-up is needed because contributor-supplied real behavior proof is still missing and the patch has security/session-state blockers; automation cannot supply the contributor’s real setup proof.; Needs attention: The diff still allows credential-field values in string-encoded tool arguments to enter the identifier preservation path.; Review finding: [P1] Sanitize string arguments before identifier ex... (sha=2adb725a544cef9149a765ea1baf16729286608f)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds transcript-wide compaction identifier extraction, identifier-loss retry hints, final-cap recovery, credential-shaped filtering, and expanded compaction safeguard tests.

PR surface: Source +193, Tests +513. Total +706 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection for the review blockers: string arguments are appended raw before regex extraction, and post-cap loss only logs when recovery exceeds the cap. I did not run a live compaction repro because this is a read-only review and the PR body’s proof is unit-test-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human follow-up is needed because contributor-supplied real behavior proof is still missing and the patch has security/session-state blockers; automation cannot supply the contributor’s real setup proof.

target.title: feat(compaction): add identifier survival validation after summarization
target.body:
## Summary

Improve compaction identifier survival with transcript-wide extraction and quality-guard retry hints. Fixes two security/correctness issues found during review:

**Fix 1 — Redact credential fields from tool arguments**: `extractMessageTextForIdentifiers` now sanitizes structured tool-call arguments through `sanitizeDiagnosticPayload` before scanning for identifiers. This prevents credential-field values (`apiKey`, `token`, `password`, `secret`) from being collected as opaque identifiers and leaked into compaction summaries.

**Fix 2 — Compare against full summary including preserved turns**: `computeLostIdentifiers` now checks against `summaryWithPreservedTurns` instead of `summaryWithoutPreservedTurns`. Identifiers present in the preserved recent-turn suffix are no longer falsely reported as "lost," avoiding redundant retry-budget churn.

## Changed files

- `src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard-quality.ts` — Import `sanitizeDiagnosticPayload`, apply before `JSON.stringify(args)` at the tool-argument scan point
- `src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.ts` — Switch `computeLostIdentifiers` target from `summaryWithoutPreservedTurns` to `summaryWithPreservedTurns`; strict identifier policy now checks the full summary including preserved turns
- `src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts` — Updated credential extraction test to verify `apiKey` fields are redacted, not extracted


## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: (1) credential-shaped values from tool arguments leak into compaction summaries via identifier extraction, and (2) identifiers present in preserved turns are falsely reported as lost, wasting the quality-guard retry budget.

Real environment tested: macOS 15.5 arm64, Node v22.19.0, pnpm v11.2.2, patched branch `compaction-identifier-survival-validation`

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the focused vitest suite that imports the production compaction safeguard modules and exercises the full identifier extraction, credential redaction, lost-identifier computation, quality-guard retry, and summary capping paths:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix:

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.7 /private/tmp/fix-75336

 ✓ |agents-core| ../../src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts (114 tests) 3339ms
 ✓ |agents-support| ../../src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts (114 tests) 3983ms

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  228 passed (228)
   Start at  04:30:55
   Duration  13.71s (transform 4.68s, setup 426ms, import 5.83s, tests 7.32s, environment 0ms)
```

Key tests verifying the fixes:

- **"redacts credential fields from tool call arguments before extraction"**: sends a tool call with `apiKey: "sk-proj-abc123def456"` and `baseUrl: "https://api.acme.io/v1"`; asserts the API key is NOT present in extracted text (sanitized via `sanitizeDiagnosticPayload`) while the URL is preserved.
- **"extracts identifiers from tool call arguments"**: verifies non-credential tool arguments (URLs, paths, session keys) are still extracted as opaque identifiers.
- **All 228 tests pass** across both `agents-core` and `agents-support` shards, including identifier extraction, quality-guard retry, summary capping, recent-turn preservation, and structured summarization.

Observed result after fix: Credential-field values (`apiKey`, `token`, `password`, `secret`) are redacted via `sanitizeDiagnosticPayload` before identifier extraction. Identifiers present in preserved turns are checked against `summaryWithPreservedTurns` (not `summaryWithoutPreservedTurns`), so they are no longer falsely flagged as "lost."

What was not tested: Live compaction with a real model and conversation history (requires a running OpenClaw session with enough context to trigger compaction). The tests import and exercise the actual production modules through vitest.
target.author: sebtardif

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
